name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
who_it_helps: Anyone in emotional distress, crisis, or thinking about suicide; available 24/7
description: Free, confidential crisis support by call or text, any time, day or night.

name: National Runaway Safeline
who_it_helps: Young people who are homeless, have run away, or feel unsafe; available 24/7
description: Free, confidential 24/7 crisis line and support for youth and young adults who are unsafe, on the streets, or thinking about running.

name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
who_it_helps: Anyone experiencing abuse or an unsafe relationship; available 24/7
description: Free, confidential 24/7 support for people experiencing domestic or dating violence, including help making a safety plan.
contact: Call 1-800-799-7233, or text START to 88788 | Web: thehotline.org

name: Hope Center for Former Foster Youth
who_it_helps: Young people who are in or have aged out of foster care
description: Programs and support for former foster youth, including help with housing stability and life skills.

name: United Way 211
who_it_helps: Anyone in the U.S. who needs to find local services
description: A free, confidential service that connects people to local resources like housing, food, and financial assistance.

name: Fostering Bright Futures
who_it_helps: Former foster youth ages 16-25 attending Wake Tech
description: A student success program at Wake Tech offering case management, mentoring, and financial support for former foster youth.
location: Raleigh, NC (Wake Tech Southern Wake Campus)

name: Medicaid for Former Foster Youth
who_it_helps: Young adults under 26 who aged out of foster care
description: Free continuous Medicaid health coverage for young adults who were in foster care and receiving Medicaid at age 18.

name: Haven House Services (Coordinated Entry)
description: The primary Coordinated Entry site for young adults (18-24) facing homelessness in Wake County to complete intake and access housing.

name: Wrenn House
description: A 24-hour crisis shelter for runaway, homeless, or in-crisis youth providing a safe environment and case management.
contact: Phone: 919-832-7866 | Address: 908 W. Morgan Street, Raleigh, NC

name: Housing Authority of the County of Wake
description: Manages public housing units and provides Section 8 housing choice vouchers to help subsidize rent for eligible individuals.

name: Wake County Food and Nutrition Services (FNS)
description: State assistance program providing EBT cards for food purchasing (formerly known as food stamps).
contact: Phone: 1-866-719-0141 | Address: 220 Swinburne St., Raleigh, NC

name: NC LINKS Program
who_it_helps: Foster youth ages 13-21 transitioning to adulthood
description: Provides social workers to help foster youth develop independent living skills, housing, education, and finances.
contact: Phone: 919-212-7000 (Ask for Wake County DSS LINKS coordinator)

name: SaySo (Strong Able Youth Speaking Out)
who_it_helps: Youth ages 14 to 24 who are or were in out-of-home care
description: A statewide association in NC providing peer support networks, transition resources, and advocacy for foster youth.

name: Oak City Cares
description: A multi-services hub providing weekend meals, showers, laundry, computers, and connection to housing resources.
contact: Phone: 984-344-9599 | Address: 1430 S. Wilmington Street, Raleigh, NC

name: The Green Chair Project
description: A nonprofit that provides gently used furniture and essential household items for people moving into new housing.

name: NCWorks Career Center (Swinburne)
description: Provides adult career services, resume assistance, job placement, and workforce training resources.
contact: Phone: 919-250-3770 | Address: 220 Swinburne Street, Raleigh, NC

name: Transition in Place (TIP) Program
description: Provides safe housing, intensive case management, and rental subsidies (up to 12 months) via Haven House.
contact: Phone: 919-833-3312 (Access via Haven House Street Outreach)

name: NC Reach Scholarship
who_it_helps: Former NC foster youth attending public college
description: A state-funded scholarship covering tuition and living expenses for up to 4 years at NC public universities or community colleges.

name: Education Training Voucher (ETV) Program
who_it_helps: Former foster youth enrolled in post-secondary education
description: A federally funded grant offering up to $5,000 per year for college or vocational training.

name: South Wilmington Street Center
description: An emergency and transitional shelter providing overnight beds, case management, and budgeting assistance.
contact: Phone: 919-857-9428 | Address: 1420 S. Wilmington St., Raleigh, NC

name: Cornerstone Center
description: A multiservice center providing crisis intervention, mental health/substance abuse services, and identification/mail assistance.
contact: Address: 220 Snow Ave., Raleigh, NC (Walk-in daytime hours)

name: Salvation Army of Wake County
description: Provides free daily evening meals and emergency assistance for the local community.
contact: Phone: 919-834-6733 | Address: 1863 Capital Boulevard, Raleigh, NC

name: Wake County DSS - Foster Care & Vital Documents
description: Can assist former foster youth in obtaining copies of birth certificates and providing case records for transition.
contact: Phone: 919-212-7000 | Address: 220 Swinburne St., Raleigh, NC
